zh.lifeSummary: 當James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry於1520年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克拉斯賀巴瑞時，他的父親Richard De Barry20歲，母親Isabella Fitzgerald15歲。他於大約1545年與Ellen MacCarthy Reagh在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克拉斯賀巴瑞結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1581年4月10日在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克拉斯賀巴瑞享年61歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 度布林格拉斯聶文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry于1520年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克拉斯賀巴瑞时，他的父亲Richard De Barry20岁，母亲Isabella Fitzgerald15岁。他于大约1545年与Ellen MacCarthy Reagh在爱尔兰庫迪 科克拉斯賀巴瑞结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1581年4月10日在爱尔兰庫迪 科克拉斯賀巴瑞享年61岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 度布林格拉斯聶文。

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the Irish name Barra . Since the 20th century this name has become very popular in the English-speaking world, especially Australia.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Barry .

nameMeanings.name: roe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly Australian: probably originally a short form of Rachel , but now generally taken as a feminine form of Ray or Raymond , or simply a derivative of ray meaning ‘sunbeam’. In some cases it may be a transferred use of the Scottish surname Rae, originally either a short form of MacRae (from a Gaelic personal name meaning ‘son of grace’) or a nickname from the roebuck. It is often used in combinations such as Rae Ellen and Mary Rae.

historicEvents.description: Henry VIII dies, succeeded by the boy king Edward VI. England and Ireland were ruled by the senior nobility of England.

en.lifeSummary: When James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry was born in 1520, in Rathbarry, County Cork, Ireland, his father, Richard De Barry, was 20 and his mother, Isabella Fitzgerald, was 15. He married Ellen MacCarthy Reagh about 1545, in Rathbarry, County Cork, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 April 1581, in Rathbarry, County Cork, Ireland, at the age of 61, and was buried in Glasnevin, County Dublin, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry est né en 1520, à Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlande, son père, Richard De Barry, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Isabella Fitzgerald, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Ellen MacCarthy Reagh environ 1545, à Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 10 avril 1581, à Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlande, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Glasnevin, County Dublin, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry 1520 in Rathbarry, County Cork, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard De Barry 20 und seine Mutter Isabella Fitzgerald 15. Er hat um 1545 in Rathbarry, County Cork, Irland Ellen MacCarthy Reagh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 10. April 1581 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Rathbarry, County Cork, Irland und wurde in Glasnevin, County Dublin, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry è nato il nel 1520 nel Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda, il suo padre, Richard De Barry, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Isabella Fitzgerald, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Ellen MacCarthy Reagh circa 1545, nel Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 10 aprile 1581, nel Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Glasnevin, County Dublin, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barryが1520年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクラスバーリーで生まれた時，父Richard De Barryは20歳，母Isabella Fitzgeraldは15歳であった。約1545年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクラスバーリーでEllen MacCarthy Reaghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1581年4月10日にアイルランドコウントイ コルクラスバーリーで61歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ダブリングラスネヴィンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry(이)가 1520년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 래스바리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Barry(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Fitzgerald(은)는 15세였다. 그는 대략 1545년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 래스바리에서 Ellen MacCarthy Reagh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1581년 4월 10일 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 래스바리에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 드블린 글라스네빈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry nasceu em 1520, em Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, Richard De Barry, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Fitzgerald, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Ellen MacCarthy Reagh aproximadamente 1545, em Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de abril de 1581, em Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Glasnevin, County Dublin, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry родился в 1520 г. в местности ратбарри, коунти корк, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Richard De Barry было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Fitzgerald, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen MacCarthy Reagh около 1545 г. в местности ратбарри, коунти корк, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 апреля 1581 г. в местности ратбарри, коунти корк, Ирландия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности гласневин, коунти дублинь, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James barry roe viscount Buttevant Barry 4th Viscount Buttevant 17th Baron Barry nació en 1520, en Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda, su papá, Richard De Barry, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Isabella Fitzgerald, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Ellen MacCarthy Reagh aproximadamente 1545, en Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 10 de abril de 1581, en Rathbarry, County Cork, Irlanda, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Glasnevin, County Dublin, Irlanda.
